description: The `EcdsaP256Signer` struct doc and the `try_sign` impl both call `p256::ecdsa::Signature::from_slice`, which validates not only byte length (64) but also that each scalar r and s is in [1, n-1] per the ecdsa crate's `from_scalars`. The public doc on `EcdsaP256Signer` (line 175-176) only documents the format constraint (64 bytes, r||s, big-endian), omitting the range constraint. A caller whose backend returns a 64-byte response with r=0 or s=0 — degenerate but byte-length-correct — will receive an unexplained `signature::Error` with no hint in the adapter docs. The same gap exists in the secretx-core `SigningBackend::sign` byte-format docstring for ECDSA P-256. Fix: add a sentence to `EcdsaP256Signer`'s struct doc noting that each scalar must be in [1, n-1] (per ECDSA spec); invalid values are rejected by `from_slice` with a `signature::Error`.
title: EcdsaP256Signer doc omits scalar-range validity constraint
